We need to build the Customers & Suppliers Invoicing Workflow as a controlled accounting cycle.

Important:
Do not rebuild the whole system.
Do not regenerate unrelated files.
First inspect the existing accounting modules, database models, routes, and pages.
Reuse existing components and patterns.

Required Modules:

1. Customer Invoices / Sales Invoices

* Create customer invoice
* Select customer
* Invoice date
* Due date
* Invoice number auto-generated
* Currency
* Payment terms
* Add invoice lines:

  * Item / service
  * Description
  * Quantity
  * Unit price
  * Discount
  * Tax
  * Total
* Invoice status:

  * Draft
  * Posted / Approved
  * Partially Paid
  * Paid
  * Cancelled
* Allow attachment upload
* Allow invoice PDF preview/print
* Allow barcode / QR code on invoice
* Link invoice to customer account statement
* Post accounting journal entry only when invoice is approved/posted, not while draft.

Accounting impact for customer invoice:
Debit: Accounts Receivable / Customer
Credit: Sales Revenue
Credit: VAT Output Tax, if applicable

2. Supplier Bills / Purchase Invoices

* Create supplier bill
* Select supplier
* Bill date
* Due date
* Supplier invoice number
* Internal reference number
* Currency
* Payment terms
* Add bill lines:

  * Item / service
  * Description
  * Quantity
  * Unit cost
  * Discount
  * Tax
  * Total
* Bill status:

  * Draft
  * Posted / Approved
  * Partially Paid
  * Paid
  * Cancelled
* Allow attachment upload
* Allow invoice PDF preview/print
* Link bill to supplier account statement
* Post accounting journal entry only when bill is approved/posted, not while draft.

Accounting impact for supplier bill:
Debit: Expense / Purchases / Inventory, depending on selected line type
Debit: VAT Input Tax, if applicable
Credit: Accounts Payable / Supplier

3. Payment Workflow
   For customer invoices:

* Record customer receipt
* Select invoice(s)
* Payment method:

  * Bank
  * Cash treasury
  * Cheque
  * Card / transfer
* Allow partial payment
* Automatically update invoice status.

Accounting impact:
Debit: Bank / Cash
Credit: Accounts Receivable / Customer

For supplier bills:

* Record supplier payment
* Select bill(s)
* Payment method:

  * Bank
  * Cash treasury
  * Cheque
  * Transfer
* Allow partial payment
* Automatically update bill status.

Accounting impact:
Debit: Accounts Payable / Supplier
Credit: Bank / Cash

4. Credit Notes
   Customer credit note:

* Reduce customer invoice value
* Link to original invoice
* Post accounting entry.

Supplier debit note / supplier credit adjustment:

* Reduce supplier bill value
* Link to original bill
* Post accounting entry.

5. Account Statements
   Customer statement:

* Opening balance
* Invoices
* Receipts
* Credit notes
* Closing balance
* Aging report

Supplier statement:

* Opening balance
* Bills
* Payments
* Debit/Credit notes
* Closing balance
* Aging report

6. Aging Reports
   Customer aging:

* Current
* 1–30 days
* 31–60 days
* 61–90 days
* Over 90 days

Supplier aging:

* Current
* 1–30 days
* 31–60 days
* 61–90 days
* Over 90 days

7. Controls

* Draft invoices must not affect accounting.
* Posted invoices cannot be deleted.
* Posted invoices can only be cancelled by reversal entry.
* Payment cannot exceed outstanding invoice balance.
* Supplier bill cannot be paid unless posted.
* Customer receipt cannot be recorded against draft invoice.
* Every posted transaction must create journal entry automatically.
* Every journal entry must be linked to its source document.
* Add audit log for create, approve, cancel, and payment actions.

Build this in phases:

Phase 1:
Database models and API routes only for customer invoices and supplier bills.

Phase 2:
Frontend pages for creating and listing customer invoices and supplier bills.

Phase 3:
Posting logic and automatic journal entries.

Phase 4:
Receipts, payments, partial settlement, and status updates.

Phase 5:
PDF print template, barcode/QR, account statements, and aging reports.

Before implementation, show me:

* Existing files you found
* What models/routes/pages already exist
* Your implementation plan
* Files you will modify
  Then wait for confirmation before coding.
